cities.name: Lehigh Acres
cities.intro: Sonny's Landscaping & Tree Services is based right here in Lehigh Acres, FL. We mow, trim, mulch, install sod, and handle tree trimming and removal across every Lehigh ZIP — backed by FNGLA Certified Horticultural Professional credentials.

faqs.question: What areas of Lehigh Acres do you cover?
faqs.answer: Every Lehigh Acres ZIP — 33936, 33971, 33972, 33973, 33974, and 33976 — plus Buckingham and Alva-adjacent properties. We're based off 28th St SW so response times across Lehigh are short.

faqs.question: Do you offer weekly mowing in Lehigh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Weekly is our most popular schedule April through October when St. Augustine is actively growing. Bi-weekly fits well November through March when growth slows. Each visit includes mowing, edging, line trimming, and blowdown.

faqs.question: Can you trim and remove palms?
faqs.answer: Yes — sabal, queen, royal, and Christmas palms are all routine. We follow the FNGLA pruning standard and never cut above the horizontal line of the fronds, which is what weakens or kills palms over time.

faqs.question: How fast can you get out for a quote?
faqs.answer: Usually within a few days. Storm cleanup and dangerous-tree calls get prioritized. Call or text (239) 265-0439.

cities.name: Fort Myers
cities.intro: Sonny's brings full-service landscaping and tree work to every Fort Myers ZIP — weekly maintenance, palm and tree trimming, mulch, sod, and full landscape design from a Lehigh-based crew that knows the area.

faqs.question: Do you handle Fort Myers commercial properties too?
faqs.answer: Yes — small offices, retail centers, condo associations, and HOA common areas. We can set up weekly or bi-weekly routes and quote tree work, mulch, and seasonal color separately.

faqs.question: Can you trim mature oaks and royal palms in McGregor?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — that's a regular part of our Fort Myers work. We use the right cuts for each species and never over-prune. Quotes are based on tree count, height, and access.

faqs.question: How is your landscape design process?
faqs.answer: A free on-site consult, then a plant list and layout matched to your soil, light, and irrigation. Install is typically scheduled within 2–3 weeks once the design is approved.

cities.name: Cape Coral
cities.intro: Sonny's serves all of Cape Coral with weekly lawn maintenance, hedge and palm trimming, tree trimming and removal, mulch, sod, and full landscape installs — including salt-tolerant plantings for waterfront properties.

faqs.question: Do you service waterfront homes including the south Cape?
faqs.answer: Yes — every Cape Coral ZIP, including Cape Harbour, Tarpon Point, Burnt Store, Pelican, and Sandoval. Salt-tolerant plant selection is part of our default approach for waterfront properties.

faqs.question: Can you remove a hurricane-damaged tree?
faqs.answer: Yes. Damaged or leaning trees get assessed on-site before quoting so the safest takedown plan is used. We grind stumps below grade and haul off debris on most removals.

faqs.question: Same-week storm cleanup in Cape Coral?
faqs.answer: Yes — storm and wind damage gets prioritized in our schedule. Call or text (239) 265-0439.

cities.name: Bonita Springs
cities.intro: Sonny's covers Bonita Springs from Bonita Beach to Worthington — weekly maintenance, palm and tree trimming, mulch, sod, and full landscape design, with salt-tolerant plant selection for coastal homes.

faqs.question: Do you really service every neighborhood in Bonita Springs?
faqs.answer: Yes — we run regular weekly routes throughout Bonita Springs for both residential and commercial properties. Whether it's a single-family lot, a condo association, or a small commercial site, we'll quote it.

faqs.question: How much does landscaping cost in Bonita Springs?
faqs.answer: A standard weekly mow on a quarter-acre lot in Bonita Springs runs $45–$85. Tree trimming, mulch, and full landscape installs are quoted on-site after a free estimate.

faqs.question: Can you do same-week service in Bonita Springs?
faqs.answer: Same-week quotes are typical for Bonita Springs. Storm cleanup, palm trimming, and tree removal usually fit within a few days. Call or text (239) 265-0439 and we'll respond directly.

faqs.question: Are you licensed and insured?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sonny's is a licensed and insured Florida landscaping business, and we hold Certified Horticultural Professional credentials through UF's FNGLA program.

faqs.question: Do you handle tree removal as well as trimming?
faqs.answer: Yes. We trim, raise, thin, and fully remove trees — including stump grinding on most jobs. We assess any storm-damaged or leaning tree before quoting so the safest removal plan is used.

cities.name: Estero
cities.intro: Sonny's services Estero with weekly lawn maintenance, palm and tree work, mulch, sod, and landscape design — covering Coconut Point, Grandezza, West Bay, and the Corkscrew Road corridor.

faqs.question: Do you really service every neighborhood in Estero?
faqs.answer: Yes — we run regular weekly routes throughout Estero for both residential and commercial properties. Whether it's a single-family lot, a condo association, or a small commercial site, we'll quote it.

faqs.question: How much does landscaping cost in Estero?
faqs.answer: A standard weekly mow on a quarter-acre lot in Estero runs $45–$85. Tree trimming, mulch, and full landscape installs are quoted on-site after a free estimate.

faqs.question: Can you do same-week service in Estero?
faqs.answer: Same-week quotes are typical for Estero. Storm cleanup, palm trimming, and tree removal usually fit within a few days. Call or text (239) 265-0439 and we'll respond directly.

faqs.question: Are you licensed and insured?
faqs.answer: Yes — Sonny's is a licensed and insured Florida landscaping business, and we hold Certified Horticultural Professional credentials through UF's FNGLA program.

faqs.question: Do you handle tree removal as well as trimming?
faqs.answer: Yes. We trim, raise, thin, and fully remove trees — including stump grinding on most jobs. We assess any storm-damaged or leaning tree before quoting so the safest removal plan is used.

blogPosts.title: Palm Tree Trimming in Southwest Florida: What to Know
blogPosts.excerpt: Over-trimming palms can permanently damage them. Here's the right way to trim queens, sabals, and royal palms in Lee County.
